description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen encased in a rectangular wooden cabinet. The front features a prominent display area bordered by a thick, rounded bezel, and a control panel on the right side with several rotary dials, push buttons, and a speaker grille. The wood grain finish gives it a classic, mid-20th-century appearance, while the back is made of vented metal for heat dissipation and houses a power input and other connectors. The television is boxy and deep, with a protruding rear section typical of cathode ray tube (CRT) technology. The base includes a small platform or foot for stability. This type of television would commonly be found in living rooms, garages, or even discarded on urban sidewalks.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood grain cabinet, convex glass screen, and a side control panel featuring dials and buttons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, wood bezel, and detailed control panel with dials and buttons on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wood grain finish and the depth of the television's cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vented metal back panel, power input, and a label, with no screen or controls visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wood grain finish and the protruding metal back, with the screen edge just visible.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, rounded wood bezel with a brown finish and metallic trim, classic vintage style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the front right edge, arranged vertically.
